2605.22776.title: SDPM: Survival Diffusion Probabilistic Model for Continuous-Time Survival Analysis
2605.22776.paper_summary: Survival analysis aims to estimate a time-to-event distribution from data with censored observations. Many existing methods either impose structural assumptions on the hazard function or discretize the time axis, which may limit flexibility and introduce approximation errors. We propose the Survival Diffusion Probabilistic Model (SDPM), a generative approach to continuous-time survival analysis. SDPM models the conditional distribution of the survival outcome, represented by the pair of observed time and censoring indicator, $\mathbb{P}(T,δ\mid \mathbf{x})$, using a denoising diffusion model. Under the assumption of conditionally independent censoring, conditional samples generated by the model can be transformed into survival function estimates using the Kaplan-Meier estimator. This formulation avoids parametric assumptions on the event-time distribution and does not require a discretization of the output time space. The model operates in a transformed target space, using standardized log-times and a continuous Gaussian-mixture representation of the censoring indicator. We evaluate SDPM on ten real survival datasets and compare it with five strong baselines, including tree-based, boosting-based, and neural survival models. Results show that SDPM achieves competitive predictive performance across C-index, integrated time-dependent AUC, and integrated Brier score. A study on synthetic Cox-Weibull data demonstrates that SDPM can recover the shape of an underlying continuous survival distribution more accurately than a strong nonparametric baseline when sufficiently many samples are generated. An ablation study confirms the importance of the proposed target-space transformations, which improve event-rate calibration, reduce invalid generated times, and provide consistent gains in predictive discrimination. Codes implementing the proposed model are publicly available.
2605.22776.paper_summary_zh: 生存分析旨在從具有截尾觀測值的數據中估計事件發生的時間分佈。許多現有的方法要麼對危險函數施加結構性假設，要麼對時間軸進行離散化，這可能限制靈活性並引入近似誤差。我們提出了生存擴散概率模型（SDPM），這是一種連續時間生存分析的生成方法。SDPM 模型化生存結果的條件分佈，由觀察到的時間和截尾指標的對組成，$\mathbb{P}(T,δ\mid \mathbf{x})$，使用去噪擴散模型。在條件獨立截尾的假設下，模型生成的條件樣本可以使用 Kaplan-Meier 估計器轉換為生存函數估計。這種公式避免了對事件時間分佈的參數假設，並且不需要對輸出時間空間進行離散化。該模型在轉換的目標空間中運行，使用標準化的對數時間和截尾指標的連續高斯混合表示。我們在十個真實生存數據集上評估了 SDPM，並將其與五個強基準進行比較，包括基於樹的、基於提升的和神經生存模型。結果顯示，SDPM 在 C 指數、綜合時間依賴 AUC 和綜合 Brier 分數方面達到了具有競爭力的預測性能。對合成 Cox-Weibull 數據的研究表明，當生成的樣本數量足夠時，SDPM 能夠比強大的非參數基準更準確地恢復潛在連續生存分佈的形狀。一項消融研究確認了所提出的目標空間轉換的重要性，這些轉換改善了事件率的校準，減少了無效生成的時間，並在預測區分上提供了一致的增益。實現所提出模型的代碼已公開可用。
2605.22776.author: Stanislav R. Kirpichenko et.al.
2605.22776.authors: Stanislav R. Kirpichenko, Andrei V. Konstantinov, Lev V. Utkin
